01 / OVERVIEW

Vertical Overview

Our multidisciplinary detailed design engineering division acts as a bridge between conceptual engineering and real-world construction. We take process drawings (PFDs and P&IDs) and develop full 3D models, layout drawings, stress analyses, and fabrication packages to ensure your refinery, petrochemical plant, or offshore asset is built exactly to spec. Our engineers coordinate across piping, mechanical, process, electrical, instrumentation, and civil/structural disciplines to prevent clashes and field delays.

Business Value Delivery

By utilizing advanced 3D modeling and dynamic stress analysis, we identify and resolve up to 98% of design clashes and piping stress load violations prior to fabrication. This reduces field rework by up to 25%, shortens construction schedules by weeks, and significantly lowers structural and raw steel material waste.

Key Differentiators

  • Integrated Aveva E3D and PDMS modeling for full visual coordination.
  • Caesar II piping stress checks mapped directly onto structural models.
  • Automated NDT and weld-tracking metadata generation for fabricators.
02 / CAPABILITIES

Specialized Workflows

Process Simulation & Safety Sizing

We develop HYSYS process models, calculate hydraulic balances, and size pressure safety relief valves (PSVs) and headers to protect personnel and assets.

Piping Stress Analysis

Using Caesar II, we simulate thermal expansion, seismic loads, and nozzle movements, sizing piping supports and expansion loops to ASME B31.3 codes.

Civil & Structural Steel Design

We engineer structural pipe racks, equipment foundations, and access platforms using STAAD Pro, ensuring structural stability under wind and dynamic loads.

03 / SECTORS SERVED

Industries Supported

Oil & Gas Refineries
Petrochemical Plants
Chemical Processing
LNG Terminal Operations
Subsea & Offshore Platforms
04 / EXECUTION TIMELINE

Project Delivery Stages

Interactive tracking of progress gates from bid to delivery handovers.

Stage 01 / Data Collection & P&ID Review

We review process design packages, capture site coordinates, and verify tag lists.

Stage 02 / 3D Layout & Modeling

Piping routes, structures, and mechanical equipment are laid out in E3D / SP3D.

Stage 03 / Stress & Structural Analysis

Piping stress runs and structural calculations validate thermal and weight loads.

Stage 04 / Fabrication Drawings Issue

Spool isometrics, structural shop drawings, and bill of materials (BOM) are issued.

05 / TECH ECOSYSTEM

Software Stack & Toolsets

Aveva PDMS / E3D

3D Plant Design

Caesar II

Piping Stress Analysis

PV Elite

Vessels Sizing

STAAD Pro

Structural Engineering

SmartPlant 3D

Plant Modeling

AutoCAD

Drafting & Layouts
06 / CASE STUDY

Execution in Practice

Brownfield Pipe Rack Expansion

The Challenge

A major petrochemical refinery needed to run a new 16" hydrocarbon line through a congested pipe rack, but legacy blueprints were inaccurate and risked clashes with active process columns.

Our Approach

We deployed terrestrial 3D laser scanners to capture a millimetre-accurate point cloud of the rack. We registered this cloud directly into Aveva E3D, modeling the new line and testing structural capacity under thermal loads in Caesar II.

The Result

The new pipe spool was fabricated offsite and installed with zero field clashes. The scan-to-model workflow saved an estimated 12 days in field installation and prevented expensive pipeline shut-downs.

07 / EXECUTED PROJECTS

Gujarat, India

LNG Vaporizer Spool Fabrication Package

08 / QUESTIONS & ANSWERS

Frequently Asked Questions

What design standards does Enconsta adhere to?

We work to global codes including ASME B31.3 (Process Piping), ASME Section VIII (Pressure Vessels), API 610/650, AISC for structural steel, and regional safety design norms.

How do you handle brownfield design accuracy?

We combine laser scan point clouds with legacy documentation to build an exact 3D visual copy, ensuring that new spools connect perfectly to existing nozzles.

What deliverables are provided in the final design package?

We deliver 3D CAD databases, piping isometrics, structural steel fabrication drawings, piping support drawings, data sheets, and a complete Bill of Materials (BOM).

Can you validate vendor-supplied equipment drawings?

Yes, we review vendor models and data sheets, checking nozzle orientations, nozzle loads, and skid footprints against our main plant model.
